# UC-22.14.76 · Risk Response Effectiveness After Control Changes

## Description

Compares alert rates before and after control deployments for RA-7 risk response monitoring.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NIST 800-53 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Tag rules consistently during pilots; (2) seasonally adjust baselines; (3) leadership narrative in monthly report; (4) document residual risk.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `` `notable` `` with `rule_name` tags for control initiatives.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Tag rules consistently during pilots; (2) seasonally adjust baselines; (3) leadership narrative in monthly report; (4) document residual risk.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
`notable` earliest=-60d tag="control_initiative_X"
| bin _time span=7d
| stats count by _time
| eventstats first(count) as baseline, latest(count) as latest
| eval reduction_pct=round(100*(baseline-latest)/baseline,1)
```

Understanding this SPL

**Risk Response Effectiveness After Control Changes (RA-7)** — Compares alert rates before and after control deployments for RA-7 risk response monitoring.

Documented **Data sources**: `` `notable` `` with `rule_name` tags for control initiatives. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Invokes macro `notable` — in Search, use the UI or expand to inspect the underlying SPL.
• Discretizes time or numeric ranges with `bin`/`bucket`.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by _time** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• `eventstats` aggregates the pipeline (counts, distinct values, sums, percentiles, etc.) into fewer rows.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **reduction_pct** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the ESP or perimeter firewall log (for example Palo Alto or FortiGate) for the same window. Spot-check a few source events, reconcile counts, and verify field extractions, thresholds, and index permissions for the people who need this evidence.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Time chart (notables per week), Single value (reduction %)

## SPL

```spl
`notable` earliest=-60d tag="control_initiative_X"
| bin _time span=7d
| stats count by _time
| eventstats first(count) as baseline, latest(count) as latest
| eval reduction_pct=round(100*(baseline-latest)/baseline,1)
```

## Visualization

Time chart (notables per week), Single value (reduction %). Key fields: `reduction_pct`.
